reviewer.name: Independent relocation reviewer
reviewer.independenceStatement: I did not implement the correction under review and evaluated this stack against the pinned requirements and the architecture status registry rather than accepting implementation claims.

currentArchitectureReview.architectureStatus: Complete Draft (document edit complete; implementation evidence pending; not ratified)
currentArchitectureReview.rationale: I read the complete Architecture 0.8 and acknowledge its status boundary: Complete Draft, document edit complete, implementation evidence pending, and explicitly not ratified (the status registry records no ratified architecture). The Reference stack locally targets Architecture 0.7; Plan 0.3 is Planned and the 0.7 delivery ledger limits the claim to tested R1-R2 Complete Draft evidence, leaves R3-R5 planned, and makes no 0.7 conformance or ratification claim. The R6 handoff accurately records Architecture 0.8 as future work, including the C7 supersession where structural three-valued evaluation replaces the delivered 0.7 composite-poisoning rule (BR-07-CONSTRAINT-001/-002/-003 becomes conflicting rework) and the section 11 revocation-ceiling representation choice, and maps C1-C14 to the 0.8 adversarial vectors. This documentation relocation changed no source and no semantics, so the retained Architecture 0.5 correction evidence stays consistent with that boundary. Assessment: current deltas are recorded; this is not an Architecture 0.8 conformance claim.

gate.notes: Substantiated reproducibility at c7c2eb4 by running verify-text, verify-doc-links, verify-evidence, verify-adversarial-vectors, verify-channel-vectors, verify-project-graph, verify-binding-measurements, verify-assembly-graph, and verify-sdk (all exit 0), and building both stacks warning-free (Reference .sln and Minimal .slnx, 0 warnings / 0 errors). In-process evidence suites executed green at this commit: Reference Core.Tests 30, Conformance 18, Extensions.Tests 3; Minimal Conformance 24. The full .\build\verify-interchange.ps1 (which also runs the cross-process interchange suites and the independent-review completeness step) completes once this review closure is committed; these two attestations are the input that step requires.

requirements.summary: Canonical names parse ConceptPath and AuthorityPath:ConceptPath without relative or ambiguous notation.
requirements.matrixRationale: The parser implements both canonical forms; a stable negative conformance test is not yet assigned.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 30); positive source anchor present, no dedicated negative test assigned
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Positive source anchor is reachable at Reference/src/Brontide.Reference.Core/Names.cs; CanonicalName parses ConceptPath and AuthorityPath:ConceptPath and rejects relative or ambiguous notation. The matrix honestly records status implemented with no separate negative conformance anchor assigned. The relocation changed no source; Reference.Core.Tests (30 passed) and a warning-free build confirm the parser at this commit. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Vocabulary versions are claims rather than semantic identity; only Shapes and Declared Fragments carry structural versions.
requirements.matrixRationale: Operation and Event identities are name-only while Shape and Fragment references carry structural versions.

positiveEvidence.anchor: public readonly record struct OperationReference(CanonicalName Name)

testsRun: Reference.Conformance ShapeConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 18)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Positive anchor OperationReference(CanonicalName Name) is reachable in Names.cs and negative Same_name_versions_are_additive_and_redefinition_is_rejected passed in Reference.Conformance (18 passed). Operation and Event identities are name-only while Shape and Fragment references carry structural versions, so vocabulary versions are claims, not semantic identity. Source byte-identical to the prior conforming review. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Actor and authority references are issuer-controlled, unambiguous, stable, and comparable within an authority domain.
requirements.matrixRationale: Actor references are domain-issued objects with no public constructor and object-reference comparison.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests ActorReferenceTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 30)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Both anchors are reachable in ActorReferenceTests.cs; typeof(ActorReference).GetConstructors() Is.Empty proves no public constructor, and the opaque, reference-comparable test confirms domain-issued, stable, comparable references. Passed in Reference.Core.Tests (30 passed); source unchanged by the relocation. Conforms.

requirements.summary: An Execution explicitly presents a Capability whose holder, target, and Operation scope match the request at the target boundary.
requirements.matrixRationale: AuthorityDomain validates initiator, target, presented Capability, and Operation before dispatch.

testsRun: Reference.Conformance WorkedAttackTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 18)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Positive ExecuteAsync anchor in AuthorityDomain.cs and negative WorkedAttackTests.Production_approval_is_denied_at_the_target_boundary are reachable; the domain validates initiator, target, presented Capability holder, and Operation scope at the target boundary before dispatch. Passed in Reference.Conformance; no source change. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Every effective Capability Constraint is evaluated conjunctively and unknown or failed Constraints deny before effects.
requirements.matrixRationale: Effective constraints are evaluated across the derivation chain and unknown types deny.

testsRun: Reference.Conformance AuthorityConformanceTests + Reference.Core.Tests KernelTests passed at c7c2eb4
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: I read the negative test Unknown_constraint_denies_before_effect_and_is_recorded: it grants a Capability bearing an unknown ValueConstraint (Example:Unknown), executes, and the effect never runs while the denial is recorded (fail-closed). The positive KernelTests liveness-lease anchor is present. Effective constraints are evaluated conjunctively over the derivation chain; the Architecture 0.8 composite-algebra delta is recorded separately and does not weaken this retained 0.5 requirement. Passed in Conformance and Core.Tests. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Delegation changes the holder, preserves inherited target and Operation scope, appends Constraints, and records its parent and issuer.
requirements.matrixRationale: The derived constructor inherits target and root Operations and stores its parent.

negativeEvidence.anchor: Derivation can only change the holder and append Constraints

testsRun: Reference.Conformance AuthorityConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 18)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Negative source anchor Derivation can only change the holder and append Constraints is present in Authority.cs; positive Delegation_adds_constraints_and_cannot_amplify passed in Reference.Conformance. Derivation preserves inherited target and root Operations, appends constraints, and records parent and issuer. Source unchanged by relocation. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Primordial authority and later Genesis occurrences are bounded, enumerable, and attributable to domain policy.
requirements.matrixRationale: Genesis is callback-scoped and recorded; context validation and mutation share the domain transaction gate, so concurrent issuance cannot resume after deactivation. A failed occurrence atomically restores actors, capabilities, newly issued and pre-existing mutable liveness-lease state, declarations, and Shape registrations. Lease mutation is coordinated with the same gate; rolled-back leases are actively invalidated and registry membership rejects other escaped references, while runtime effects and nested Genesis are blocked during the transaction.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests KernelTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 30)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Positive GenesisOccurrences enumeration anchor is present in AuthorityDomain.cs; the three negative KernelTests anchors (failed occurrence rolls back unrecorded authority; rolls back lease renewal; concurrent issuance cannot resume after rollback) are present and passed in Reference.Core.Tests (30 passed). Genesis is bounded, recorded, and atomically rolled back on failure. Conforms.

requirements.summary: The requested effect begins only after explicit target-side authority, Shape, and Constraint validation.
requirements.matrixRationale: Handlers run after authority, Shape, and Constraint decisions.

testsRun: Reference.Conformance CoolingConformanceTests + AuthorityConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Negative anchor Assert.That(effects, Is.Zero) and positive CoolingConformanceTests.Minimal_cooling_system_runs_only_through_checked_executions are reachable and passed in Reference.Conformance; handlers run only after target-side authority, Shape, and Constraint validation. No source change. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Rejected attempts remain observable Executions while protected command payloads are excluded from denial audit records.
requirements.matrixRationale: Rejected executions retain execution metadata and decisions in provenance, but the audit copy does not retain the protected input; the direct caller's ExecutionResult remains complete.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests KernelTests + Reference.Conformance AuthorityConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Negative KernelTests.Rejected_provenance_excludes_the_protected_input is present and passed in Core.Tests: the audit provenance copy stores the execution without its protected input while the direct caller result stays complete. Rejected attempts remain observable Executions. Positive AuthorityConformanceTests anchor present. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Trusted target time is explicit, scoped to a time domain, monotonic where claimed, and distinct from attributable sender marks.
requirements.matrixRationale: TimeProvider is explicit, sender Temporal Marks never supply trusted constraint time, and an observed-expired liveness lease remains terminally dead across clock rollback.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests KernelTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 30)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Both anchors are present in KernelTests; Wall_clock_constraint_fails_closed_when_domain_has_no_clock and the terminal-death liveness test passed in Core.Tests. Trusted time is the explicit TimeProvider, sender Temporal Marks never supply constraint time, and observed expiry is terminal across clock rollback. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Events are immutable assertions that preserve emitter attribution, causation, declared Shape, and original temporal claims.
requirements.matrixRationale: Emitter, causation, origin, and temporal attribution are retained.

testsRun: Reference.Extensions.Tests (Failed: 0, Passed: 3) + Reference.Conformance OriginConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Positive ExtensionScenarioTests event-mediator and replay anchor passed in Extensions.Tests (3 passed); negative OriginConformanceTests.Direct_origin_assertion_denies_constraints_without_event_semantics passed in Conformance. Events preserve emitter, causation, declared Shape, and original temporal claims across replay. Conforms.

requirements.summary: An Outcome is a terminal Event for one identifiable Execution or activity, with distinct success result and failure detail Shapes.
requirements.matrixRationale: Outcomes are terminal Events and validate success and failure values under separate contracts.

testsRun: Reference.Conformance OutcomeConformanceTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 18)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Both OutcomeConformanceTests anchors (independent success and failure Shapes; failed.Outcome.Result Is.Null) passed in Reference.Conformance. An Outcome is a terminal Event naming one identifiable Execution with distinct result and failure-detail Shapes. Source unchanged. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Shape identity is semantic and supports unit, scalar, record, sequence, choice, and opaque contracts without CLR assignability.
requirements.matrixRationale: All Base Shape kinds are represented and semantic identity does not use CLR assignability.

testsRun: Reference.Core.Tests KernelTests passed at c7c2eb4 (Failed: 0, Passed: 30)
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: Both KernelTests anchors (registry supports all base shape kinds without CLR assignability; scalar carriers are typed, immutable, and cannot contain authority) passed in Core.Tests. Shape identity is semantic across unit, scalar, record, sequence, choice, and opaque. Conforms.

requirements.summary: Same-name Shape versions are monotonic and additive; breaking structure or meaning requires a new canonical name.
requirements.matrixRationale: Later same-name Shape definitions are checked for additive compatibility.

review.rationale: Positive additive-versions anchor and negative Throws.TypeOf<ShapeRegistrationException>() passed in Reference.Conformance; later same-name Shape versions must be additive and breaking redefinition throws. Conforms.

requirements.matrixRationale: Open, required, projected, and closed Fragment behavior has executable evidence.

requirements.summary: Cross-stack component evidence crosses an external boundary without sharing implementation-private runtime types or authority objects.
requirements.matrixRationale: The retained full gate launches the independently built Minimal provider process for Cooling and Catalog/resource proofs. This is experimental interchange evidence, not protocol ratification.

testsRun: Cross-process ReferenceHostsMinimal and Catalog suites run under full .\build\verify-interchange.ps1 (completes at review closure); verify-dependencies guard present
Gate at c7c2eb4: nine verify-*.ps1 scripts + Reference and Minimal builds all exit 0 (0 warnings)
review.rationale: The positive cross-process anchors in ReferenceHostsMinimalTests and CatalogBindingTests are reachable; the negative guard verify-dependencies.ps1 forbids any Brontide.Minimal project reference. The cross-process suites are exercised by the full verify-interchange gate (7/7 at the byte-identical prior conforming baseline; completes here once the review closure is committed). No Capability or implementation-private runtime type crosses the process boundary, so this is demonstrated experimental interchange, not ratification. Conforms.

attestation.statement: I attest that this record accurately reports my independent review of the pinned commit and the architecture selected by the status registry.
